share_basis_note: 14,725,873K diluted shares (Q2 FY26 weighted, ended 2026-03-28) x $298.01 (2026-06-18 close, last <= as_of) = ~$4.39T. Price from Yahoo chart API [S005]; 2026-06-19 intraday not used as freeze edge. Single common share class, one-share-one-vote.

module_signals.finding: Anchor quarter (Q2 FY26, ended 2026-03-28) and full-year FY25/FY24 baseline are fully primary-sourced (8-K EX-99.1 tables): Q2 revenue $111.2B/+17%, iPhone $57.0B, Services $31.0B, Greater China $20.5B/+28%, GM 49.3%, EPS $2.01/+22%, new $100B buyback; FY25 revenue $416.2B, NI $112.0B, OCF $111.5B, FCF ~$98.8B, buybacks $90.7B. Balance sheet net cash ~$61.9B. Price locked from dated daily series. Gaps (10-K line items, proxy, Google TAC magnitude, early 10yr series) are haircuts, not vetoes.

Integrated hardware+software personal-computing ecosystem anchored by a 2B+ active installed base, monetized by ~70%-margin Services (App Store, Google TAC, iCloud, Pay, AppleCare, ads, Music/TV+). Mechanism is an annuity: hardware one-time sales convert to recurring high-margin Services (FY25 Services +13.5%, Q2 +16.3%). Multi-year primary-sourced, not a story. exceptional_compounder. Theme intact; question is price and AI-era entry-point durability (Siri AI just previewed at WWDC, partly leans on Google cloud / Nvidia GPUs).

Apple captures the personal-computing profit pool via ecosystem switching cost + brand pricing power + custom SoC + App Store two-sided platform. Operator grade ~4/5: Tim Cook (~15yr tenure, disciplined capital allocation, one-share-one-vote, FY25 buybacks $90.7B + dividend raise). Durability HIGH on the installed-base annuity (no erosion; all categories at record installed base). Ceiling capped (not floored) on durability by: App Store take-rate under global antitrust/DMA pressure, Google TAC under US search-remedy threat (O1), succession/key-man on Cook (late tenure, O3), and AI-era entry-point uncertainty (O4). Annuity moat is LESS cyclical than NVDA -> durability would support Core-level size IF price allowed.

Owner earnings are unusually clean: capex-light (FY25 capex $12.7B / revenue $416B = 3%), FCF ~= net income (no NVDA-style equity-gain distortion; FY24 Ireland State Aid one-time isolated). TTM NI $122.6B, TTM FCF ~$129B, owner-earnings anchor ~$120B (conservative). Fortress balance sheet: net cash ~$61.9B. Per-share engine = ~2.5-4%/yr share shrink. Rising gross margin (43% -> 49% quarterly) as Services mix lifts. Net strongly positive on quality; the issue is the PRICE paid for it, not the earnings.

Kill paths real but NOT structural impairment at as-of: (1) valuation compression (~36x, 2.7% yield) -> a PRICE risk handled by M6, not an M5 veto; (2) Google TAC cut by search-remedy default-deal ban effective 2026-06 (~$20B/yr high-margin Services at risk, magnitude OPEN O1) -> margin erosion, haircut; (3) App Store take-rate forced down globally (DMA/antitrust) -> the ONLY path that can escalate toward structural Services-margin breakage, but needs multi-year global synchrony, installed-base moat still intact -> not a veto now; (4) iPhone cycle air-pocket + China full-year still -3.8%; (5) AI paradigm shift bypassing the ecosystem entry point (Siri late, leans on Google/Nvidia). None is permanent profit-pool loss or balance-sheet death (net cash $61.9B). This is the INVERSE of a false-cheap trap: the live risk is a false-EXPENSIVE trap = overpaying for a genuine compounder (the 2016 lesson is NOT to under-ceiling durability, but 2026 is ~36x vs 2016 ~10x, so price-capping new money is correct, not a repeat of undersize). Price, not durability, is the binding constraint.

At ~$4.39T, trailing P/OE ~36.5x, starting OE yield ~2.7%, base 5y IRR +2.7% (« 8% hurdle), price near 52-week high (-6%) -> price is the BINDING CONSTRAINT (same as GOOGL, opposite of NVDA). Asymmetry is unfavorable: bull +11% / base +2.7% / bear -7%. Net cash only 1.4% of market cap (was 31% in 2016) -> valuation cushion essentially gone. Buy-below ~$231 (8% base IRR, ~28x OE); starter zone ~$210 (~26x, 10%). New money: WATCH (0%), no-chase at/above current. Existing position: HOLD (keep compounding via buybacks, no impairment risk; do not add).

starter_zone: No starter at current price. Starter ~3-5% only on a pullback to <= ~$231 (base IRR ~8%, ~28x trailing OE).
add_zone: Scale toward 8-15% only at <= ~$210 (base IRR ~10%, ~26x). Durability (annuity moat) would support Core-level size, but ONLY if price cooperates; price, not durability, gates this.
trim_or_no_chase_zone: No-chase at/above current ~$298. Existing holders HOLD (do not trim a ten-year compounder for being expensive; do not add new money). Trim only on thesis disconfirmation (K-red: iPhone + Services both roll over, OR App Store take-rate structurally broken).
binding_constraint: Price / valuation starting point. At ~36x trailing OE and 2.7% starting owner-earnings yield, base 5y IRR is +2.7%, far below the 8% hurdle -> the business is exceptional and durable, but new money has no margin of safety. Completeness (~60%) and durability do NOT cap here; price does (the M6 price gate caps the verdict at WATCH, same mechanism as GOOGL). Secondary watch: Google TAC search-remedy magnitude (O1), AI-era entry-point durability (O4), Cook succession (O3).
kill_criteria: iPhone revenue declines YoY for 2-3 consecutive quarters AND Services growth falls below high-single-digits -> installed-base annuity bridge breaking; reassess durability (possible existing-position trim).
Google TAC materially cut by the search-remedy default-deal ban AND Services gross margin visibly compresses -> margin erosion confirmed (haircut; deepens WATCH, magnitude OPEN O1).
App Store take-rate forced structurally lower globally (DMA/antitrust) impairing high-margin Services -> the ONLY path that can escalate toward veto (F3).
Absolute installed-base decline -> ecosystem moat breakage signal.
Price stays at/above ~$298 (no MOS) -> new money remains WATCH; only a pullback to <= ~$231 unlocks STARTER.
Cook departure/incapacitation with no credible succession -> durability-ceiling watch (existing HOLD, not necessarily trim).
runner_dissent: Recorded per protocol. The opposite-error alarm (the 2016 SEVERE-UNDERSIZE lesson) fires loudest here: in 2016 the harness under-ceiled a +1318% compounder by anchoring on a 'mature/ex-growth' story, and the cost of caution was enormous. A WATCH-0% on AAPL risks the same regret IF the business out-compounds. I overrode that toward WATCH (not STARTER) ONLY because, unlike 2016, PRICE IS THE CONSTRAINT and the constraint is objective, not narrative: 2016 entry was ~10x with net cash 31% of market cap (a generational margin of safety); 2026 entry is ~36x with net cash 1.4% (the cushion is gone), and the three-scenario base 5y IRR is +2.7% vs an 8% hurdle, with asymmetry tilted DOWN (bull +11% / bear -7%). Critically, I did NOT downgrade the durability prior (it stays HIGH, which is the actual fix for the 2016 error) -- I let PRICE, not a maturity story, do the capping. The 2016 mistake was being timid when cheap; capping new money when expensive is the opposite, correct discipline, not a recurrence. Existing holders therefore HOLD (a ten-year compounder with no impairment risk and ongoing buyback accretion should not be sold for being expensive), but new money WATCH (zero margin of safety). This is the GOOGL pattern (great business, wrong price), and the inverse of NVDA (great business, friendly price ~19x / +13% base IRR). The verdict flips to STARTER the moment price reaches buy-below ~$231. Residual OPEN items left honest: Google TAC magnitude (O1), AI-entry-point monetization (O4), proxy/succession (O3), 10-K line items (O2), early 10yr series (O5, B2-tier).
